news_bbc.title: Plans to end gazumping with binding agreements in house sales shake-up
news_bbc.summary: Sales agreements will be legally binding sooner and making sellers provide more home information up front are part of the planned changes.

news_bbc.title: Warning over 'fragile' public finances as borrowing rises
news_bbc.summary: The UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, according to official figures, up almost a third on the same month last year.

news_bbc.title: 'I'd be put off if he asked to split it': Who should pay on a first date?
news_bbc.summary: Some insist on splitting the bill, others say the asker should pay, while many still see a man paying as romantic.

news_bbc.title: Brexit cost 6% of UK economy, Bank of England company data suggests
news_bbc.summary: Analysis showed how much the UK could have grown if it had not exited the EU.

news_bbc.title: Five ways the Iran peace deal could affect you and your money
news_bbc.summary: With fuel and gas prices having fallen in recent days, we look at how the end of hostilities might affect you - in five charts.

news_bbc.title: The artificial ice pyramids saving India's mountain villages
news_bbc.summary: Himalayan villages are creating artificial glaciers to guarantee water for their crops in the spring.

news_bbc.title: Interest rates held as Bank warns of impact of high energy prices
news_bbc.summary: The Bank last cut interest rates in December but upheaval in the Middle East has stalled any further reductions.

news_bbc.title: 'Do not travel' advice for Dubai dropped
news_bbc.summary: Thousands of Brits were left stranded in the Middle East when the US-Iran war broke out in early 2026.
